author: deme1744
content:
Yes, and thanks to these test vectors we already found and fixed some bugs. But more work to be done still.

I did just realize that the new release does have a bunch of test vectors that caused some false failures. Most of the added tests under `witness_validation_*` are supposed to fail stateless execution apparently. However because our tests are still only working with just the `executionWitness` and not yet the serialized `statelessInputBytes` and `statelessOutputBytes` our tests could not know. But the description made that clear.

So for now I've just quickly added the deserialization of `statelessOutputBytes` to know the result there and skip witness comparison if it is `successful_validation == false`.
This is not yet sufficient for a few tests that do have `successful_validation == true` but simply have some added unneeded witness data. So I'll have to be a bit more lenient when comparing these.

But I'll rework this soon to fully deserialize the Input also, compare it with `executionWitness` and also execute the given data (as opposed the only the one our implementation generates now).

As mentioned, the way I'm using these test vectors is to both validate our stateless execution implementation and our witness generation.

author: jsign
content: Pefect, that makes sense! Thanks for the update 🙏 Glad the tests helped surface bugs!
